 title: Navigation menu does not list the motion commands
 ---

The menu builder is not surfacing these commands. Investigate apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs menu population vs the NAV_COMMAND_SPEC / command registry — the nav commands likely lack `menu` metadata or are filtered out of the menu build. +The native Navigation menu does not show the motion/nav commands (nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/jump/drillIn/drillOut etc.) even though the keybindings work.
+
+## Investigation finding (do NOT re-derive — this redirects the fix)
+The menu BUILDER is already correct. `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` passes all 14 tests, including `navigation_submenu_contains_all_nine_nav_commands` and the accelerator tests — proving `collect_menu_entries` over the real composed registry yields a `Navigation` group with all nine `nav.*` (incl. `nav.jump`), and `resolve_accelerator` gives valid accelerators in cua and vim. The nav.yaml metadata is present and correct (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml`, each nav.* has `menu: { path: [Navigation], ... }`).
+
+So the metadata/builder hypothesis in the original card is WRONG. The real bug is BUILD-ORDER / TIMING:
+- `apps/kanban-app/src/main.rs::setup_app` calls `build_initial_menu` (~line 144) EARLY, building the OS menu from the registry as it exists at that moment.
+- The focus/nav commands get composed into the registry LATER — see `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs` registry composition (~665-669) and `reload_command_overrides` (~949), which runs during `auto_open_board`, AFTER `build_initial_menu`.
+- `reload_command_overrides` swaps the registry but does NOT call `rebuild_menu`. Nothing rebuilds the menu after the nav/focus commands land in the registry.
+- Result: the OS menu is built without the nav commands and never refreshed; meanwhile the `s`/nav KEYBINDINGS work because they resolve from the (later-populated) registry/scope at dispatch time. This is the exact asymmetry the user observed: "`s` jump works but isn't shown in navigation."
+
+## Work
+- Make the menu reflect the fully-composed registry: rebuild the menu after the nav/focus commands are registered (e.g. call `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async` after `reload_command_overrides` / after board substrate composition), OR defer/repeat `build_initial_menu` until the registry is complete. Pick the approach that guarantees the Navigation submenu is populated in the running app.
+- `menu.rs`: `build_menu_from_commands`, `collect_menu_entries`, `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async`, `update_menu_enabled_state`.
+
+## TDD (RED first)
+Write a failing test at the integration seam that exercises the REAL startup/board-open sequence (not just `collect_menu_entries` in isolation, which already passes). The test must prove that AFTER the registry is composed the way the running app composes it (initial menu build BEFORE nav/focus commands, then registry composition / `reload_command_overrides`), the menu that the app would display contains the Navigation submenu with the nine nav.* commands. The existing unit test passes because it builds the menu from an already-complete registry — the new test must reproduce the early-build-then-late-registry ordering and show the menu is stale, then go green once a rebuild is wired in.
+
+## Acceptance
+- Nav commands appear under a Navigation menu with their accelerators in the actually-running app (build order fixed).
+- New regression test reproduces the build-order bug (RED before the fix) and is GREEN after.
+- `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` stays green.
\ No newline at end of file

-## Investigation finding (do NOT re-derive — this redirects the fix)
-The menu BUILDER is already correct. `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` passes all 14 tests, including `navigation_submenu_contains_all_nine_nav_commands` and the accelerator tests — proving `collect_menu_entries` over the real composed registry yields a `Navigation` group with all nine `nav.*` (incl. `nav.jump`), and `resolve_accelerator` gives valid accelerators in cua and vim. The nav.yaml metadata is present and correct (`crates/swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml`, each nav.* has `menu: { path: [Navigation], ... }`).
+REOPENED 2026-06-06 — prior fix was WRONG and discarded. The native Navigation menu does not list the motion/nav commands (nav.up/down/left/right/first/last/jump/drillIn/drillOut), and the command palette is also missing from the OS menu.

-So the metadata/builder hypothesis in the original card is WRONG. The real bug is BUILD-ORDER / TIMING:
-- `apps/kanban-app/src/main.rs::setup_app` calls `build_initial_menu` (~line 144) EARLY, building the OS menu from the registry as it exists at that moment.
-- The focus/nav commands get composed into the registry LATER — see `apps/kanban-app/src/state.rs` registry composition (~665-669) and `reload_command_overrides` (~949), which runs during `auto_open_board`, AFTER `build_initial_menu`.
-- `reload_command_overrides` swaps the registry but does NOT call `rebuild_menu`. Nothing rebuilds the menu after the nav/focus commands land in the registry.
-- Result: the OS menu is built without the nav commands and never refreshed; meanwhile the `s`/nav KEYBINDINGS work because they resolve from the (later-populated) registry/scope at dispatch time. This is the exact asymmetry the user observed: "`s` jump works but isn't shown in navigation."
+## OWNER CORRECTION (authoritative — supersedes all prior notes)
+The CommandService MUST specify OS-menu placement. The plugins/command-service exist to REPLACE the builtin YAML (`compose_registry!` / `nav.yaml`). The OS menu must be built FROM the CommandService catalogue, and each command's `menu` (MenuPlacement) must be carried THROUGH the service catalogue.

-## Work
-- Make the menu reflect the fully-composed registry: rebuild the menu after the nav/focus commands are registered (e.g. call `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async` after `reload_command_overrides` / after board substrate composition), OR defer/repeat `build_initial_menu` until the registry is complete. Pick the approach that guarantees the Navigation submenu is populated in the running app.
-- `menu.rs`: `build_menu_from_commands`, `collect_menu_entries`, `rebuild_menu`/`rebuild_menu_async`, `update_menu_enabled_state`.
+The discarded approach (do NOT repeat): a prior attempt made `state.rs::sync_commands_registry_from_metadata` OVERLAY the builtin `compose_registry!` YAML on top of the CommandService snapshot (new `compose_facade_registry` / `overlay_defs`) to re-inject the nav menu metadata. That is inconsistent with the architecture — it props up the YAML the service is meant to replace. All of that code was reverted to HEAD.

-## TDD (RED first)
-Write a failing test at the integration seam that exercises the REAL startup/board-open sequence (not just `collect_menu_entries` in isolation, which already passes). The test must prove that AFTER the registry is composed the way the running app composes it (initial menu build BEFORE nav/focus commands, then registry composition / `reload_command_overrides`), the menu that the app would display contains the Navigation submenu with the nine nav.* commands. The existing unit test passes because it builds the menu from an already-complete registry — the new test must reproduce the early-build-then-late-registry ordering and show the menu is stale, then go green once a rebuild is wired in.
+## Correct goal
+Make commands available on the OS menu via the CommandService — including:
+- Navigation (the nine nav.* motion commands), AND
+- the command palette opener (currently absent from the OS menu entirely).
+The service catalogue is the single source of truth; the menu reads from it. If nav/focus command metadata (incl. `menu` placement) does not currently reach the service catalogue, the fix is to make it reach the service — not to overlay YAML.

-## Acceptance
-- Nav commands appear under a Navigation menu with their accelerators in the actually-running app (build order fixed).
-- New regression test reproduces the build-order bug (RED before the fix) and is GREEN after.
-- `cargo test -p kanban-app --bins menu` stays green.
\ No newline at end of file
+## Next step
+Re-investigate (architecturally-correct lens): how command `menu` placement is supposed to flow into the CommandService catalogue and out to `apps/kanban-app/src/menu.rs`; why nav.* (and the palette command) menu placement does not reach the service catalogue today; and the minimal change so the service carries it. TDD, RED first. This card is now part of the broader "commands on the OS menu" effort the owner is driving next.
\ No newline at end of file
